notes: Rapidly growing neck mass with constitutional symptoms — high malignancy concern
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 62 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Sarah Mendez, Family Medicine, (416) 555-0192

Chief complaint: Rapidly growing left neck mass, 4-week progression. Mass is firm, non-tender, fixed, approximately 4.5 cm. Patient reports unintentional 14 lb weight loss and drenching night sweats over past 6 weeks. Smoker x 35 years, 1 PPD. ETOH use social.

No imaging completed. No biopsy. CBC ordered — pending. No prior ENT history.

Concerned for malignancy. Requesting urgent evaluation.

notes: Confirmed oral carcinoma — requires urgent surgical evaluation
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 71 years old
Referring physician: Dr. James Okafor, Oral Surgery, (416) 555-0341

Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, floor of mouth, confirmed on biopsy 3 days ago.
Pathology report attached. MRI head and neck ordered — results pending.

Patient is anxious and understands diagnosis. No prior radiation or chemotherapy.
Requesting urgent ENT surgical oncology consultation for staging and treatment planning.

notes: Nasal fracture with narrow surgical window
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 24 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Alan Torres, ER, St. Michael's Hospital, (416) 555-0654

Presenting complaint: Nasal fracture from sports injury (hockey) 9 days ago. Significant dorsal deviation to the right. Edema resolving. Epistaxis resolved at scene. Patient desires surgical reduction.

CT face completed day of injury — comminuted nasal bone fracture with septal deviation. Report attached.

TIME SENSITIVE — surgical window for closed reduction closes approximately 2 weeks post-injury. Patient has been counselled.

notes: Infant tongue tie with documented failure to thrive
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 6 weeks old
Referring physician: Dr. Priya Nair, Pediatrics, (416) 555-0877

Reason for referral: Ankyloglossia (tongue tie) with significant impact on breastfeeding. Infant has lost 8% of birth weight and has not recovered to birth weight at 6 weeks. Lactation consultant has been involved — mechanical issue identified. Mother experiencing significant distress and pain during feeds.

No imaging required. Requesting urgent frenotomy assessment.

notes: Suspicious rapidly growing oral lesion — possible malignancy
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 55 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Maria Chen, Dentist, (416) 555-0123

Reason for referral: White and red mixed lesion on lateral border of tongue, right side. Lesion has grown from approximately 5mm to 12mm over 4 weeks. Patient is a 40 pack-year smoker and daily alcohol user. No pain currently, but area is indurated on palpation.

No biopsy performed. No imaging. Requesting urgent ENT evaluation for possible oral malignancy.

notes: Stable pediatric bilateral hearing loss — no urgency indicators
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 7 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Priya Nair, Pediatrics, (416) 555-0877

Reason for referral: Bilateral mild sensorineural hearing loss detected on school hearing screening. Child is developmentally normal, thriving, no speech delay. Parents report no change in hearing over the past year. No ear pain, discharge, or balance issues. No feeding or swallowing concerns.

Audiogram attached showing mild bilateral SNHL at 4kHz. Requesting ENT assessment and hearing aid evaluation.

notes: Chronic sinusitis — stable, no complications
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 44 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Robert Singh, Family Medicine, (416) 555-0567

Reason for referral: Chronic recurrent right-sided sinusitis for approximately 2 years. Four courses of antibiotics in past 12 months with incomplete relief. CT sinuses from 6 weeks ago shows mild right maxillary sinus opacification. No polyps, no orbital or intracranial complications.

Patient is otherwise healthy. Requesting surgical consultation for possible functional endoscopic sinus surgery (FESS). CT report attached.

notes: Recurrent tonsillitis — standard surgical referral
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 10 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Linda Park, Pediatrics, (905) 555-0234

Reason for referral: Recurrent tonsillitis — 7 documented episodes in the past 12 months meeting Paradise criteria. No peritonsillar abscess. No obstructive sleep apnea documented. Otherwise healthy child.

Requesting tonsillectomy assessment. No imaging required. No acute illness at time of referral.

notes: Non-growing small oral lesion — stable, benign appearance
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 38 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Kevin Huang, Dentist, (416) 555-0789

Reason for referral: Small whitish lesion (fibroma) on inner cheek mucosa, 3mm, noted for approximately 1 year with no change in size. Non-tender. Smooth surface. No adjacent lymphadenopathy. Non-smoker.

Requesting routine ENT evaluation to confirm benign nature and determine if excision warranted.

notes: Stable ear pain — no infection signs, no urgent features
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 28 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Sandra Williams, Family Medicine, (416) 555-0432

Reason for referral: Intermittent right ear pain for 3 months. No discharge, no hearing loss, no vertigo. Otoscopy normal in office. Tympanogram normal. No jaw pain or dental issues. Tried NSAIDs with partial relief.

Requesting ENT evaluation to rule out TMJ vs. referred pain etiology.

text:
Please refer this patient to ENT.

The patient has had a growth in their mouth for a while. Please assess.

No additional information available.

notes: Sudden unilateral hearing loss — treated as URGENT per guidelines
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 45 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Christine Lau, Family Medicine, (647) 555-0321

Reason for referral: Sudden onset right-sided hearing loss 3 days ago. Patient woke up with complete absence of hearing in right ear. No associated pain. Mild tinnitus. No prior hearing issues. No trauma, no infection.

Requesting URGENT ENT evaluation for sudden sensorineural hearing loss. Steroid window is time-sensitive.

notes: Rapidly growing neck mass in child — high suspicion lymphoma
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 12 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Michelle Foster, Pediatrics, (905) 555-0110

Chief complaint: Bilateral neck lymphadenopathy rapidly enlarging over 6 weeks. Largest node 3 cm on right, 2.5 cm on left. Child has had fever, fatigue, and significant unintentional weight loss. Splenomegaly noted on exam.

CBC: WBC elevated 18.2, lymphocytes elevated, blasts on smear (report attached).

High suspicion for lymphoma. Requesting urgent ENT and hematology co-assessment.

notes: Nasal polyps — stable, elective surgical assessment
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 52 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Nelson Obi, Family Medicine, (416) 555-0876

Reason for referral: Known bilateral nasal polyps on CT sinuses from 4 months ago. Patient has been on intranasal steroids for 18 months with partial symptom control. No acute sinusitis. No anosmia. Stable course.

Requesting ENT assessment for possible polypectomy. CT report attached. No labs required.

notes: Post-operative follow-up — routine care transition

REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Female, 35 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Anita Desai, Family Medicine, (416) 555-0543

Reason for referral: Post-operative follow-up after septoplasty performed at outside institution. Patient is 6 weeks post-op. No complications. Mild residual nasal congestion improving. Requesting ongoing ENT management. Operative report from previous surgeon attached.

notes: Stridor in adult — airway concern
text:
REFERRAL — ENT

Patient: Male, 67 years old
Referring physician: Dr. Paul Greenwood, ER, Humber River Hospital, (416) 555-0987

Reason for referral: New onset inspiratory stridor for 2 days, progressive. No fever, no drooling. Long-standing smoker. History of external radiation to neck 10 years ago for laryngeal cancer (in remission per patient). Voice quality changed over past month.

CT neck with contrast ordered in ER — results pending. Requesting urgent ENT evaluation for possible airway or recurrence concern.
